name: Voting Router
description: FastAPI router providing voting endpoints for tools. Allows authenticated users to vote for tools and remove their votes. Enforces one vote per user per tool and only allows voting on active tools.

types.name: VoteResponse

fields.name: vote_count
fields.description: Total number of votes for the tool after this vote

types.description: Response returned when a vote is successfully cast

types.name: AlreadyVotedResponse
types.description: Response when user has already voted for this tool

types.name: RemovedVoteResponse

fields.name: vote_count
fields.description: Total number of votes for the tool after removal

types.description: Response when vote is successfully removed

types.name: NotVotedResponse
types.description: Response when user attempts to remove a non-existent vote

functions.name: vote_useful
functions.description: Allows an authenticated user to cast a vote for a tool by slug. Creates a ToolVote record if the user has not already voted. Returns vote status and updated vote count.

inputs.name: slug
inputs.description: Tool slug identifier from URL path parameter

error_cases.name: tool_not_found
error_cases.condition: Tool with given slug does not exist OR tool.is_active == False

preconditions: User must be authenticated (enforced by get_current_user dependency)
postconditions: If tool exists and user has not voted: ToolVote record created and committed to database
If tool exists and user has not voted: Returns {'status': 'voted', 'vote_count': <count>}
If user has already voted: Returns {'status': 'already_voted'} without modifying database
Vote count reflects the current total votes for the tool
side_effects: Executes database SELECT query to find Tool by slug and is_active=True
Executes database SELECT query to check for existing ToolVote
If vote is new: Creates and inserts ToolVote record into database
If vote is new: Commits database transaction
Executes database COUNT query to retrieve total vote count

structured_side_effects.description: Multiple async database queries and potential write
Creates new ToolVote record when user has not previously voted

functions.name: remove_vote
functions.description: Allows an authenticated user to remove their vote for a tool by slug. Deletes the ToolVote record if it exists. Returns removal status and updated vote count.

inputs.name: slug
inputs.description: Tool slug identifier from URL path parameter

error_cases.name: tool_not_found
error_cases.condition: Tool with given slug does not exist OR tool.is_active == False

preconditions: User must be authenticated (enforced by get_current_user dependency)
postconditions: If tool exists and user has voted: ToolVote record deleted and committed to database
If tool exists and user has voted: Returns {'status': 'removed', 'vote_count': <count>}
If user has not voted: Returns {'status': 'not_voted'} without modifying database
Vote count reflects the current total votes for the tool after removal
side_effects: Executes database SELECT query to find Tool by slug and is_active=True
Executes database SELECT query to find existing ToolVote
If vote exists: Deletes ToolVote record from database
If vote exists: Commits database transaction
Executes database COUNT query to retrieve total vote count

structured_side_effects.description: Multiple async database queries and potential delete
Deletes ToolVote record when user has previously voted

invariants: Router is mounted at /api/tools prefix with 'voting' tag
All endpoints require authenticated user (via get_current_user dependency)
Only active tools (is_active=True) can be voted on or have votes removed
Each user can have at most one vote per tool (enforced by business logic, not database constraint in this code)
Vote count queries are executed after write operations to ensure consistency

data_access.rationale: Reads Tool records to validate tool exists and is active. Reads and writes ToolVote records to manage user voting state.

side_effects.rationale: Required to validate tool existence and active status before allowing vote operations
Prevents duplicate votes by checking for existing vote before creation
Records user vote in ToolVote table
Removes user vote from ToolVote table
Returns current vote count to client after mutation
